mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling in equities within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances during late-cycle expansions over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength, driving sustained equity appreciation over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: Joint exceedance of moving averages fails to produce a positive mean forward return over 60 days, with t-statistic below 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that outweigh demand, causing negative equity returns as earnings growth stalls.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles yield positive 20-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS slope is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation acceleration alongside stable interest rates creates a real rate compression shock, prompting immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities within one week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.4% while FEDFUNDS remains flat
failure_condition: The specified CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS configuration produces positive 5-day returns in >58% of historical instances, invalidating the real rate compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a reliable leading indicator of corporate revenue expansion, generating positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% triggers recession fears that immediately suppress equity valuations, resulting in negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 declines by more than 0.5%, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production surges during a post-shock recovery phase signal rapid capacity utilization and earnings rebound, driving sharp short-term equity rallies.
failure_condition: INDPRO surges in recovery regimes fail to generate positive 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 10 trading days.
